title: Russet Roasted Japanese Potato Udon
description: A lazy-sunday Japanese dish from Chubu region featuring crispy double-fried potatoes, udon noodles, and a rich cheesy sauce, making it a shojin-friendly option for a comforting meal. This peppery and savory dish is perfect for a meal that's both easy on the palate and the stomach.
instructions: Peel and cut Russet potatoes into 1-inch cubes and double-fry them in batches until crispy, about 4-5 minutes each batch.
Cook udon noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large saucepan, melt unsalted butter over medium heat and whisk in heavy cream (if using).
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan cheese until melted and smooth.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Add soy sauce (if using) and stir to combine.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of cooked udon noodles on a plate, top with a layer of crispy potatoes, and drizzle with the cheese sauce.
Garnish with grated daikon and chopped scallions (if using).
